title: C++ Strings
content:
### Strings in C++

C++ supports two types of strings: C-style strings (character arrays) and the `std::string` class from the Standard Library.

---

### std::string (Recommended)

```cpp
#include <string>

string name = "John";
string greeting = "Hello, " + name; // Concatenation
```

---

### Common String Operations

| Operation | Code | Result |
|-----------|------|--------|
| Length | `str.length()` or `str.size()` | Number of characters |
| Access | `str[0]` | First character |
| Append | `str.append("!")` or `str += "!"` | Modified string |
| Insert | `str.insert(3, "abc")` | Insert at position |
| Erase | `str.erase(3, 2)` | Remove 2 chars from pos 3 |
| Find | `str.find("abc")` | Position or `string::npos` |
| Substring | `str.substr(0, 3)` | First 3 characters |
| Compare | `str1 == str2` | true if equal |

---

### Input with Strings

```cpp
string name;
cin >> name;        // Reads one word

getline(cin, name); // Reads entire line including spaces
```

---

### C-Style Strings

```cpp
char greeting[] = "Hello";
char name[20] = "John";

#include <cstring>
strcpy(dest, src);   // Copy
strlen(str);         // Length (excluding null)
strcmp(a, b);        // Compare
```

---

> **Key Point:** Always prefer `std::string` over C-style strings. It is safer, more convenient, and automatically manages memory.
